note: LLM in the loop: Claude (claude-opus-4-8) ranked the vetted rescue paths and the executor sent its choice to a live EVM. The deterministic core sized every path and enforced the spend cap + action whitelist BEFORE ranking, so the model's only freedom was which safe path to take.
scenario: mEURC collateral, USDC debt; EURC drifts down until HF crosses the Mandate trigger

viablePaths.why: TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold)
DELEVERAGE: sell 2.24 USDC of mEURC, repay debt

ranking.memo: HF 1.120 < trigger 1.200. Target 1.380. Debt 5.00 USDC. Candidates: TOP-UP, ROTATE, DELEVERAGE. LLM ranked 3 viable paths and chose TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold) (cost 0.00 USDC). Reason: TOP-UP restores health from your prepaid reserve with no swap, no slippage, and no collateral sold, at zero cost now — the cheapest durable fix versus rotating (two swap fees) or deleveraging (0.36 USDC and a permanently smaller position).
